# UC-22.13.42 · Incident Evidence Preservation — Splunk Search Artifact Export Audit

## Description

We audit exports of saved searches and large result downloads during active incidents to support evidence preservation and chain-of-custody discipline.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes _audit. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Maintain active incident lookup updated by IR. (2) Tune `total_count` threshold. (3) Pair with proxy DLP if used. (4) Retain audit logs per records program. Indexes required: `index=_audit`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `_audit` index `action=search` `info=granted`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain active incident lookup updated by IR. (2) Tune `total_count` threshold. (3) Pair with proxy DLP if used. (4) Retain audit logs per records program.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=_audit action=search info=granted earliest=-30d total_count>50000
| lookup cip008_active_incidents.csv search_name OUTPUT incident_id
| where isnotnull(incident_id)
| stats sum(total_count) as rows values(user) as users by incident_id, search_name
| sort - rows
```

Understanding this SPL

**Incident Evidence Preservation — Splunk Search Artifact Export Audit (CIP-008-6 R5 Part 5.1)** — We audit exports of saved searches and large result downloads during active incidents to support evidence preservation and chain-of-custody discipline.

Documented **Data sources**: `_audit` index `action=search` `info=granted`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: _audit.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=_audit, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(incident_id)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by incident_id, search_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP firewall log, Windows Security, or the asset register—whichever the search is built to mirror. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (large searches), Bar chart (by user), Single value (export events)

## SPL

```spl
index=_audit action=search info=granted earliest=-30d total_count>50000
| lookup cip008_active_incidents.csv search_name OUTPUT incident_id
| where isnotnull(incident_id)
| stats sum(total_count) as rows values(user) as users by incident_id, search_name
| sort - rows
```

## Visualization

Table (large searches), Bar chart (by user), Single value (export events). Key fields: `incident_id`, `rows`, `search_name`, `users`.
